meta_title: For Students: SEO for SaaS to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SEO for SaaS and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: SEO for SaaS: A Student's Guide to Growth & Portfolio
content.seo_description: Break past basic app limitations. Learn how to implement SEO for SaaS using Antigravity and Claude Code in this comprehensive beginner checklist.
content.answer_block: Stop building apps that nobody finds. By integrating server-side rendering, semantic metadata, and automated sitemap generation, you transform a 'basic project' into a discoverable product. Use Antigravity to structure your architecture and Claude Code to automate technical SEO implementation, ensuring your portfolio gets the organic traffic it deserves.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding out code, perfecting your UI, and squashing bugs, only to launch into the void. Your app is solid, but it’s invisible. The 'basic app' syndrome isn't a failure of your engineering skills; it's a failure of discoverability. Most students treat SEO as an afterthought, but in the real world, the best code is useless if the search engines can't index your value prop.

It’s time to move from 'coder' to 'product builder.' By treating SEO as a first-class technical feature, you turn your portfolio into a growth engine. We’ll use Antigravity to scaffold high-performance structures and Claude Code to refine your technical metadata, effectively turning your side project into a searchable, professional SaaS asset.

steps.title: Architecting for Crawlability
steps.description: Ensure your app structure is readable by bots by implementing proper semantic HTML5 and server-side rendering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SEO-friendly Next.js layout with semantic HTML5 tags, ensuring critical content is rendered on the server for search engine crawlers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement semantic headers and main tags in layout.tsx'

steps.title: Dynamic Metadata Injection
steps.description: Stop using static titles. Use dynamic metadata to ensure every page on your app targets a specific high-intent keyword.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a metadata generator function that pulls dynamic titles and descriptions from the database for each route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'update page.tsx files to include dynamic metadata generation'

steps.title: Automated Sitemap Generation
steps.description: Search engines need a roadmap. Automate your sitemap to update whenever you deploy new features or content.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a sitemap.xml script that automatically crawls all active routes and outputs a valid XML file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add sitemap.ts to app directory and configure dynamic route indexing'

steps.title: Schema Markup Implementation
steps.description: Use JSON-LD to give search engines structured data about your SaaS, boosting your chance of rich snippets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a JSON-LD schema helper for a SaaS application, including SoftwareApplication markup for search visibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'inject SoftwareApplication JSON-LD script into the root layout head'

steps.title: Performance-Based SEO Audit
steps.description: SEO is tied to load speed. Use technical tools to ensure your site hits a 90+ Lighthouse score.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase for performance bottlenecks and optimize image loading and script execution for faster LCP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'optimize image components and lazy load non-critical scripts'

pro_tips: Always prioritize 'Long-tail' keywords in your metadata; they have lower competition and higher conversion for student projects.
Use canonical tags even on small apps to prevent duplicate content issues during local development and deployment.
A sitemap is good, but a robots.txt file that specifically allows your primary content paths is the true gatekeeper.
Optimize your 'Open Graph' images; they are the primary driver of CTR when your project is shared on social media.
Track your SEO performance early with Google Search Console; don't wait until you have 1,000 users.
content.next_steps: Once your SEO architecture is live, submit your sitemap to Google Search Console and begin writing three 'Problem-Solution' blog posts to link back to your landing page.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to make your code count. Stop building in silence and start building for scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
